title: campanula mollis subsp. microphylla
intro: Campanula mollis subsp. microphylla is a subspecies of the Campanula mollis plant, commonly known as the soft bellflower, a low-growing perennial plant with delicate, bell-shaped flowers and soft, hairy leaves. Campanula mollis subsp. microphylla is used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin, wounds, and respiratory issues. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including colds, flu, and coughs, due to its expectorant and antiviral properties, which help to relieve congestion and reduce the severity of symptoms. Campanula mollis subsp. microphylla can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies.
